D1_Justification: EAc-RE Renewable Energy: 5/110 pts (~4.5%). On-site PV explicitly listed as qualifying system using GHG-based metric not % energy. Pages 135-142.
D2_Justification: No distinction between BIPV and BAPV; EAc-RE uses generic 'on-site renewable energy systems'; photovoltaic array named but no building-integration distinction. Pages 135-142.
D3_Justification: Only rooftop PV typically counted; facade PV can contribute to energy model but no specific pathway. IPc1 (p.14-17) encourages but does not require solar analysis.
D4_Justification: No mandatory solar study; IPc1 Integrative Process encourages but does not require solar feasibility. Pages 14-17.
D5_Justification: 1-3% energy from renewables for max 5 pts; no net-zero requirement in base system; Exemplary Performance at 100% but not required.
D6_Justification: Commissioning required (EAc3-5); no ongoing PV-specific performance verification. Pages 135-142.
D7_Justification: PV only counts in Energy & Atmosphere category; no crossover to Materials or envelope credits.
D8_Justification: Innovation credit available for exemplary renewable energy performance (100% on-site RE).

D1_Justification: Ene01 up to 9+5 credits for carbon reduction (~11% of weighted 100); Ene04 LZC feasibility 1-3 credits. Criterion 7 p.146 for LZC energy offset. Pages 144-175.
D2_Justification: No explicit BIPV term in Ene01 or Ene04; treats all LZC technologies generically. Ene04 p.169 lists eligible LZC technologies under Directive 2009/28/EC but no BIPV/BAPV distinction.
D3_Justification: Feasibility study considers all renewables including PV; roof PV most common; facade mentioned indirectly via novel LZC tech (SBEM-Q approval needed). Ene04 pages 167-175.
D4_Justification: Ene04 Criterion 9 MANDATORY LZC feasibility study at Concept Design stage. Must consider all LZC technologies including PV. Pages 167-175.
D5_Justification: Full Ene01 exemplary credits require net-zero and carbon-negative beyond; Criterion 9 very ambitious. Pages 144-155.
D6_Justification: Post-construction commissioning; Ene01 Criteria 10-12 require post-occupancy energy monitoring for 2 additional credits. Pages 150-155.
D7_Justification: PV reduces carbon in Energy category; minor link to envelope thermal modeling via whole-building carbon modeling.
D8_Justification: Innovation credit for exemplary net-zero and net-positive carbon performance (Ene01 Criteria 8-9).

D1_Justification: Renewable Energy section: up to 20 bonus points (~20% of 100-point base). ZE/PE tiers require 100%/115% on-site RE. Stage 1 & 2 Verification mandatory for new buildings. Pages 7-10.
D2_Justification: BIPV mentioned in SLE programme guidance; solar feasibility study references facade PV; ZE/PE tiers require maximizing generation area (implying facade use). No explicit BIPV credit ID but SLE recognises facade BIPV.
D3_Justification: Roof PV primary; facade BIPV recognised in SLE programme; ZE/PE tiers effectively require facade surfaces. Pages 7-10.
D4_Justification: Solar feasibility study mandatory via Stage 1 & 2 Verification process in GM:2021; design-stage integration required. Pages 7-10.
D5_Justification: SLE requires significant RE contribution (60% energy savings minimum); ZE = 100%; PE = 115% onsite. Not all buildings required to meet ZE/PE.
D6_Justification: Post-occupancy energy audit mandatory (Stage 2 Verification); actual PV output verified over 12 months. Pages 7-10.
D7_Justification: PV primarily in Energy Efficiency; some crossover with envelope in SLE programme.
D8_Justification: Innovation points for exceeding RE targets; ZE/PE tiers reward novel BIPV integration.

D1_Justification: Energy category ~25 points for GHG reduction on 100-point scale; PV contributes via carbon modeling.
D2_Justification: No specific BIPV credit; PV treated generically within whole-building carbon modeling.
D3_Justification: Roof PV standard; facade PV can contribute to carbon model but no dedicated pathway.
D4_Justification: No mandatory solar study; integrated design encouraged but not prescriptive for PV.
D5_Justification: 6-Star World Leader effectively requires net-zero carbon; PV essential for top tier.
D6_Justification: As-built verification required; operational performance via NABERS separate.
D7_Justification: PV counted in Energy; minor interaction with Materials for lifecycle.
D8_Justification: Innovation Challenge credits can reward novel BIPV integration or net-positive energy.

D1_Justification: Section 5.2.16 Renewable energy: ~10/400 total points (~2.5%). Three RE categories assessed: domestic hot water, cooling/heating, electricity (PV counts toward electricity ratio Re). Pages 25-26 and 90.
D2_Justification: No BIPV distinction; generic renewable energy utilization. RE defined as wind/solar/water/biomass/geothermal/ocean (p.13). No building-integration distinction.
D3_Justification: Roof PV standard; solar thermal mentioned; no specific facade BIPV pathway. Section 5.2.16.
D4_Justification: No mandatory solar feasibility study. Section 5.2.16 is a performance credit only.
D5_Justification: Requires >=1% electricity RE for credits (max 10 pts at higher %); modest target. Pages 25-26.
D6_Justification: Operational assessment (运行评价) requires operational data for verification. Section 5.2.16 pages 88-91.
D7_Justification: PV only in Energy Saving section; no crossover.
D8_Justification: Innovation bonus items (加分项) exist but not specifically for BIPV.

D1_Justification: EU5a Solar Feasibility Study: 1 credit; EU5b Renewable Energy Application: 5+5 bonus credits out of ~120 (~4-8%). Pages 312-317.
D2_Justification: EU5a explicitly names BIPV in credit scope: 'Consideration of PV, BIPV or solar thermal potential installation' (EU5a criterion 1, p.313). Only system in dataset with explicit BIPV credit language.
D3_Justification: Roof PV dominant; EU5a scope includes 'standalone and building-integrated installation'; high-rise facade PV mentioned in guidance. Pages 312-317.
D4_Justification: EU5a Solar Energy Feasibility Study earns 1 credit; encouraged but not mandatory prerequisite for EU5b. Must be endorsed by Locally Qualified Professional. Page 313.
D5_Justification: Full base credits at only ~2% energy from renewables; very low ambition. EU5b max 10 pts at 2% RE. Pages 315-317.
D6_Justification: Commissioning verification; no ongoing operational M&V for PV specifically. BEAM Plus In-Use handles existing buildings.
D7_Justification: PV only counts in Energy Use section.
D8_Justification: Bonus credits for exceeding 2% RE; generic innovation credit pathway.

D1_Justification: LR1.4.3 Expanding Adoption of Non-Fossil Energy: renewable energy sub-items within LR1 (~3-4% equivalent via BEE ratio). SDG 7.2.1 explicitly lists solar power. Pages 131, 134-135, 145.
D2_Justification: No BIPV distinction; renewable energy treated generically. PV described as 'converted use of natural energy' (LR1.3). SDG 7.2.1 cited but no BIPV/BAPV distinction. Pages 131-135.
D3_Justification: Roof PV standard; limited facade PV discussion. LR1.4.3 lists solar power generically. Pages 134-135.
D4_Justification: No mandatory solar feasibility study requirement. LR1.4.3 is optional scoring sub-item.
D5_Justification: Level-based scoring; modest renewable contribution expected for BEE improvement. Pages 131-145.
D6_Justification: Operational data used in CASBEE for Existing Building separate tool. CASBEE-BD 2024 requires post-occupancy data via DECC. Pages 256-259.
D7_Justification: PV within energy load reduction (LR factors); no crossover to Q quality factors.
D8_Justification: No specific innovation pathway for renewable energy in CASBEE-BD.

D1_Justification: ENV1.1 LCA includes PV lifecycle; ENV2.1 Primary Energy; lifecycle-oriented. EN 15316-4-3 for thermal solar and PV systems referenced (p.61). Pages 1-10.
D2_Justification: BIPV recognised through LCA methodology - facade LCA explicitly included. EN 15316-4-3 covers thermal solar and PV systems. Facade BIPV assessed as building component lifecycle. Pages 1-10, 61.
D3_Justification: Roof PV standard; facade BIPV explicitly discussed in LCA methodology; building envelope as energy surface included. Pages 1-10.
D4_Justification: Energy concept required at design stage; LCA renewable study part of integrated planning. ENV1.1 requires LCA in planning phase. Pages 1-5.
D5_Justification: Significant primary energy reduction required; nearly-zero-energy as reference; EU EPBD aligned; Agenda 2030 bonus for net-positive. Pages 5-10.
D6_Justification: Lifecycle performance verification; ENV1.1 requires operational monitoring data for full LCA credit. Pages 7-10.
D7_Justification: BIPV contributes to ENV1.1 LCA + TEC technical envelope + ECO lifecycle cost — genuine multi-category integration. Page 61.
D8_Justification: Innovation area for exemplary renewable integration; Agenda 2030 bonus for excess energy generation. Pages 5-10.

D1_Justification: No direct renewable energy or PV credits; focused on health and wellbeing.
D2_Justification: No mention of BIPV or PV technology.
D3_Justification: No PV application type recognized.
D4_Justification: No solar or energy generation study required.
D5_Justification: No renewable energy ambition; energy only addressed indirectly via thermal comfort.
D6_Justification: No PV performance verification.
D7_Justification: No envelope-energy synergy for PV.
D8_Justification: No innovation pathway for PV/BIPV.

D1_Justification: Energy section: renewable energy ~8/100 points (~8%). Note: Only 5th Addendum (2016) reviewed; full manual not available.
D2_Justification: No BIPV distinction; on-site renewable energy including solar. Full manual data not available from addendum.
D3_Justification: Roof PV primary pathway; no specific facade or window PV recognition. Based on existing matrix data.
D4_Justification: No mandatory solar feasibility study. Based on existing matrix data.
D5_Justification: Minimum 5% energy from renewables for credits.
D6_Justification: Post-occupancy monitoring encouraged but not strictly mandated for PV.
D7_Justification: PV only in Energy category.
D8_Justification: Innovation credit available but generic.

D1_Justification: RE-R1 (mandatory RE feasibility) and RE-1 to RE-3: renewable energy ~8-10% of total credits. RE-3 max 7 pts (feasibility + 1-25% energy tiers). Pages 97-101.
D2_Justification: No specific BIPV language; on-site renewable energy systems. Solar photovoltaics named in RE-3 submission documents but no BIPV/BAPV distinction. Pages 97-101.
D3_Justification: Roof PV primary; shading PV mentioned in hot climate context. RE-3 eligible technologies include solar electricity and solar thermal. Pages 97-101.
D4_Justification: Energy audit and RE assessment required via RE-R1 (mandatory requirement). RE-3 performance method has feasibility component.
D5_Justification: Requires minimum 3% on-site RE for lowest RE-3 tier; max credits at 25% onsite RE; aligned with Abu Dhabi sustainability goals. Pages 97-101.
D6_Justification: Post-occupancy energy performance monitoring required for Pearl-rated buildings. Pages 97-101.
D7_Justification: PV in Resourceful Energy category only. No envelope synergy documented.
D8_Justification: Innovation credit for exceeding RE targets.

D1_Justification: Energy category: renewable energy sub-criteria ~5% of total. Existing matrix data retained.
D2_Justification: No BIPV distinction; generic renewable energy.
D3_Justification: Roof PV standard; no facade or window PV pathway.
D4_Justification: No mandatory solar study.
D5_Justification: Modest renewable energy contribution targets.
D6_Justification: Post-occupancy data collection encouraged.
D7_Justification: PV in Energy only.
D8_Justification: Innovation possible but not PV-specific.

D1_Justification: Section 3.4.1 On-Site Renewable Energy: 30/1000 pts (~3%). Feasibility study 5 pts + on-site RE % up to 25 pts. Pages 205-211.
D2_Justification: No BIPV distinction; generic renewable energy. Section 3.4.1 does not name solar PV specifically nor distinguish BIPV/BAPV. Pages 205-211.
D3_Justification: Roof PV primary; no specific facade recognition. Section 3.4.1 lists 'on-site renewable energy systems' generically. Pages 205-211.
D4_Justification: Feasibility study optional but earns 5 pts (3.4.1.1). No mandatory solar study; self-assessment approach. Pages 205-211.
D5_Justification: Points for on-site renewable percentage (5-100%+ of energy cost); modest targets accessible. Pages 208-209.
D6_Justification: Commissioning and verification included in Sections 3.7-3.8. No PV-specific ongoing M&V. Pages 22-24.
D7_Justification: PV in Energy only.
D8_Justification: Continuous improvement pathway (Section 3.4.1.3 encourages exceeding targets).

D1_Justification: Energy category: renewable energy integration ~5-8% of total. Note: Only Certivea taxonomy alignment report (2023) reviewed; full HQE scheme manual not available.
D2_Justification: No specific BIPV language; references French RE2020 regulation which includes PV. Partial data from taxonomy report.
D3_Justification: Roof PV standard; French RE2020 increasingly requires facade considerations. Partial data.
D4_Justification: Energy study required as part of French building permit; includes renewables assessment. Based on existing matrix data.
D5_Justification: RE2020 compliance pushes toward significant renewable contribution; lifecycle carbon approach. Based on existing matrix data.
D6_Justification: Post-completion energy audit.
D7_Justification: Some crossover with envelope thermal via RE2020 lifecycle approach.
D8_Justification: Innovation points available.

D1_Justification: Energy section: renewable energy contributes to 20% energy savings threshold.
D2_Justification: No specific PV or BIPV language; focuses on % energy savings.
D3_Justification: Roof PV standard pathway; no facade distinction.
D4_Justification: No mandatory solar study; simplified certification for emerging markets.
D5_Justification: 20% minimum energy savings target; PV is one pathway among many.
D6_Justification: Self-reported performance; no ongoing M&V.
D7_Justification: PV only contributes to energy savings percentage.
D8_Justification: No innovation credit.

D1_Justification: Imperative I-08 Net Positive Carbon (Energy Petal): 105% net-positive energy MANDATORY from on-site RE only. I-07 requires 70% energy reduction first. Pages 42-45.
D2_Justification: All on-site renewable recognised; BIPV encouraged for aesthetic integration via Beauty Imperative I-19. I-08 'on-site renewable energy' allows any form of PV including building-integrated. Pages 42-45.
D3_Justification: Roof and facade PV both needed to achieve net-positive mandatory requirement; building envelope treated as energy surface. I-08 pages 42-44.
D4_Justification: Integrated energy study inherent in pursuing net-positive; design charrette required. I-07 and I-08 together require full energy strategy. Pages 43-44.
D5_Justification: 105% net-positive energy from on-site RE only is MANDATORY — most ambitious of any system reviewed. Pages 42-44.
D6_Justification: 12 months of actual performance data required before certification granted. Continuous monitoring via meters. Pages 44-45.
D7_Justification: PV contributes to Energy Petal (I-08) and Materials Petal (I-12 lifecycle); genuine multi-category integration. Pages 42-45.
D8_Justification: Entire system is innovation-driven; Beauty Imperative I-19 encourages BIPV aesthetics; Scale Jumping allows off-site RE expansion. Pages 42-45.

D1_Justification: PER (Primary Energy Renewable) factor: Plus requires ≥60 kWh/m²a on-site RE generation; Premium requires ≥120 kWh/m²a (relative to building footprint). Classic tier has no generation requirement. Section 2.1 Table 1, pages 10-16.
D2_Justification: No specific BIPV language; PV generation counted toward PER balance generically. Rear-ventilated solar collectors/PV panels excluded from cool colour criterion (Section 3.2.9, p.34). No BIPV/BAPV distinction.
D3_Justification: Roof PV standard; facade PV can contribute to PER calculation but no specific pathway or incentive. Section 3.2.9, pages 33-34.
D4_Justification: No mandatory solar study; focus is on envelope and airtightness first. PHPP calculation implicitly requires PV sizing for Plus/Premium. Pages 10-16.
D5_Justification: Plus requires 60 kWh/m²a RE generation; Premium requires 120 kWh/m²a — significant for low-energy buildings with PHPP basis. Section 2.1 Table 1.
D6_Justification: Blower door test mandatory; PHPP energy balance verified; energy monitoring encouraged for Plus/Premium. Section 2.1.
D7_Justification: PV generation offsets primary energy via PHPP tool; interaction with envelope U-values and shading in energy balance. Pages 10-34.
D8_Justification: Premium tier rewards maximum renewable generation; entire system incentivises envelope-first then PV.

D1_Justification: Criterion 8 Renewable Energy Utilization: max 5 pts/100 (5%). Mandatory 5% RE for daytime-occupancy buildings. Innovation Criterion 30 for net-positive energy pathway. Pages 77-79.
D2_Justification: BIPV listed in abbreviations (p.17): 'BIPV = Building-Integrated Photovoltaics'. Not explicitly named in Criterion 8 text but abbreviation recognition suggests awareness. Criterion 28 mentions solar photovoltaic panels.
D3_Justification: Roof PV primary; Criterion 28 mentions 'renewable energy systems: solar photovoltaic panels' generically. No facade or window PV pathway.
D4_Justification: No mandatory solar study. Criterion 8 is performance-based.
D5_Justification: Minimum 5% RE mandatory for daytime-occupancy buildings; max 5 pts at 25% RE. Innovation Criterion 30 for net-positive energy. Pages 77-79.
D6_Justification: Post-occupancy monitoring encouraged via Criterion 8 and GRIHA operations framework.
D7_Justification: PV in Energy Optimisation category only.
D8_Justification: Innovation Criterion 30 includes net-positive energy; generic innovation credit available. Pages 77-79.

D1_Justification: Credit E-6 Renewable Energy: 2/55 pts (~3.6%). 1 pt at 0.5%, 2 pts at 1.5% of building energy from on-site renewables. Pages 66-68.
D2_Justification: No BIPV language in E-6 or elsewhere in reviewed sections. PV listed generically as 'Photovoltaic (PV) & Solar Thermal'. Pages 66-68.
D3_Justification: Roof PV assumed standard; no other application types mentioned. E-6 lists valid RE forms but no application type distinction. Pages 66-68.
D4_Justification: No mandatory solar study; E-6 is straightforward performance credit. Pages 66-68.
D5_Justification: Very modest targets (0.5-1.5% of energy). Pages 66-68.
D6_Justification: No PV-specific verification in E-6. Based on manual review.
D7_Justification: PV in Energy only.
D8_Justification: No specific innovation for renewables in reviewed sections. Pages 66-68.

D1_Justification: EA2 Renewable Energy: 2/85 pts (~2.4%). 1 pt at 0.5%, 2 pts at 1.5% of annual energy cost. Solar cell explicitly named. Pages 34-35.
D2_Justification: No BIPV language in EA2 or reviewed sections. Solar cell named but no BIPV/BAPV distinction. Pages 34-35.
D3_Justification: Roof PV (solar cell) assumed; no facade or application type distinction. Pages 34-35.
D4_Justification: No mandatory solar study. EA2 is straightforward performance credit. Pages 34-35.
D5_Justification: Modest targets: 0.5-1.5% of energy cost. Pages 34-35.
D6_Justification: No PV verification required in EA2. Based on manual review.
D7_Justification: PV in Energy and Atmosphere only.
D8_Justification: No innovation for renewables in EA2; GI Innovation section (5 pts) available but generic. Pages 34-35.

D1_Justification: MINERGIE-A requires net-zero energy; PV generation integral to Minergie Coefficient (MKZ) calculation. Full roof PV assumed as MKZ baseline. 2017 standard required ≥10 Wp/m² ERA. Pages 1-2.
D2_Justification: No specific BIPV term; PV generation integral to MKZ calculation. Full roof PV assumed generically. No BIPV/BAPV distinction. Pages 1-2.
D3_Justification: Roof PV standard (full roof assumed in MKZ baseline); facade PV increasingly important for A-ECO standard but no explicit credit. Pages 1-2.
D4_Justification: Energy balance calculation (MKZ) required; inherently includes PV sizing at design stage. Pages 1-2.
D5_Justification: MINERGIE-A requires net-zero energy balance; very ambitious. Plus standard: energy demand reduction. 2017 Wp/m² requirement demonstrates direct RE mandate. Pages 1-2.
D6_Justification: Operational energy monitoring required for MINERGIE certification maintenance from 1000m² ERA. Pages 1-2.
D7_Justification: PV contributes to energy balance MKZ; some interaction with envelope via MINERGIE-P standard.
D8_Justification: MINERGIE-A ECO rewards comprehensive approach; Premium tier maximises renewable generation.
